title: T-032 — Regulatory roadmap artifact under _foundation/regulatory/ with source dates and review guard
prompt:
# T-032 — Regulatory roadmap artifact under _foundation/regulatory/ with source dates and review guard

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §11, §14, §W0-v4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
ACP import shape: top-level TaskCreate only (`title`, `prompt`, `labels`, `priority`, `max_attempts`, `pipeline_name`, `pipeline_inputs`). Do not add generated ACP fields such as task_id/status/project_id. `pipeline_inputs.root_path` is mandatory.

## Goal
Create the regulatory roadmap documentation artifact and staleness guard from PRD §11 without inventing product implementation.

## Locked Wave 0 decisions to honor
- Business data-plane scope is `org_id`; do not create new business-table `tenant_id` columns. Tenant/control-plane concepts may exist above org scope, but app/business rows, RLS policies, audit/outbox rows, role grants, and R13 skeletons use `org_id`.
- Canonical finished-good domain/event prefix is `fg.*`. `fa.*` / `FA` may appear only as an explicit legacy compatibility alias during migration, never as the primary new contract.
- Use the safe non-spoofable org-context RLS pattern. Do not rely on unsafe direct custom GUC `SET` by app users. Do not declare `LEAKPROOF` unless the implementation proves it is valid and necessary.
- Lower numeric priority is picked sooner; dependencies must be local `T-XXX` IDs. Cross-module blockers, if any, belong in `pipeline_inputs.cross_module_dependencies` and in this prompt.
- Exactly one atomic task type: `T5-seed`.

## Implementation contract
Create `_foundation/regulatory/README.md` with owner role, quarterly cadence, and Wave0 naming rules (`org_id`, `fg.*`). Create one markdown file each for FSMA 204, EUDR, Peppol BE, EU ViDA, BRCGS Issue 10, EU FIC 1169/2011 + 2021/382, and Polska KSeF. Each file has YAML front matter keys `title`, `enforcement_date`, `scope_modules`, `last_reviewed_at`, `source_url`, and body sections `Scope`, `Impacted foundation contracts`, `Open questions`. Add `scripts/check-regulatory-staleness.mjs` that fails when last_reviewed_at is older than 100 days or required keys are missing. Enforcement dates must come from PRD §11; use `direction-known-date-explicitly-unset` for KSeF because the PRD says delayed/direction certain and gives no exact date.

## Files in scope
- _foundation/regulatory/README.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/fsma-204.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/eudr.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/peppol-be.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/eu-vida.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/brcgs-issue-10.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/eu-fic-1169-2011.md [create]
- _foundation/regulatory/polska-ksef.md [create]
- scripts/check-regulatory-staleness.mjs [create]

## Dependencies
- T-005

## Cross-module dependencies
- None

## Acceptance criteria
1. Seven regulation files exist and each has exactly the required front matter keys.
2. The staleness script exits non-zero for a fixture older than 100 days and for a file missing a required key.
3. README names the owner role and quarterly review cadence.
4. No enforcement date is invented beyond PRD §11; KSeF is represented as date explicitly-unset/direction-known.

## Test strategy
- Run `node scripts/check-regulatory-staleness.mjs`.
- Run a fixture or temp-file negative test for stale/missing-key behavior.
- Run markdown/front matter parse check if available.

## Risk red lines
- Do not invent enforcement dates.
- Do not build regulatory app features.
- Do not use old filename casing `polska-kseF.md`; use lowercase `polska-ksef.md`.

## Closeout evidence
Provide changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status, deviations_from_prd, and git_status.
